title: omega fails on raw Finset.card atoms without inclusion-exclusion bridging
informal_statement: A goal proving `(s ∪ t).card + (s ∩ t).card = s.card + t.card` for `s t : Finset ℕ`, attacked by `omega` directly on the four `Finset.card` atoms instead of first invoking the union/intersection cardinality identity.

failure.input_context_summary: Goal was the inclusion-exclusion cardinality identity `(s ∪ t).card + (s ∩ t).card = s.card + t.card` over `Finset ℕ`.
failure.attempt_summary: Applied `omega` directly after `intro s t` without bridging through `Finset.card_union_add_card_inter`. `omega` treated `s.card`, `t.card`, `(s ∪ t).card`, and `(s ∩ t).card` as four independent, unconstrained natural-number atoms and reported a spurious counterexample (`a + b - c - d ≥ 1` for `a := s.card`, `b := t.card`, `c := (s ∪ t).card`, `d := (s ∩ t).card`), since it has no built-in knowledge of the Finset union/intersection cardinality relationship between them.
failure.lesson: Bridge Finset cardinality identities (e.g. `Finset.card_union_add_card_inter`) into scalar equations before invoking `omega`/`nlinarith`; these tactics only see linear relations they are told about and cannot derive combinatorial identities between distinct `Finset.card` atoms on their own.

notes: Produced through the tracked proof-search MCP loop (problem 84eaf4f4-21db-4e02-986c-bb78aae7e931, episode 29b897d0-2c51-4a9b-8bb4-5f781b0a753c): a `solve` step with `intro s t; omega` returned a kernel_fail with the `omega` tactic-failure diagnostic quoted in `failure.attempt_summary`, then the episode was closed with `give_up`. First v0.1 negative example for the combinatorics domain (previously 0).
